IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2008 Continued To Mar-07-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX, L.L.C.'S, SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ABEX, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: STEPHEN TIGERMAN, ESQUIRE AND KATHERINE WANG, ESQUIRE (HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, 415.788.1588) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JANN M. NODDIN, ESQUIRE AND DONNA L. MAUL, ESQUIRE (BRYDON HUGO & PARKER, 415.808.0300) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE AND CONTINUES THE MATTER TO MARCH 7, 2008. MR. TIGERMAN IS TO SUBMIT FURTHER BRIEFING ON ISSUES SET FORTH ON THE RECORD BY MARCH 6, 2008. LATE ISSUED TENTATIVE RULING AND NOT APPLIED: The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof. Plaintiff's exposure while working on locomotives and railcars is preempted by federal law specifically the Boiler Inspection Act and the Safety Appliance Act. The decisions in Napier v. Atlantic Coast Line (1926) 272 U.S. 605; Scheiding v. General Motors (2000) 22 Cal.4th 471 and Carrillo v. ACF Industries (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1158 are persuasive authority on the question of preemption. Plaintiff's reliance on the 2007 amendment to the Federal Railroad Safety Act does not change the persuasive authority of the decided cases. The reasoning of the recent decision by the First Appellate District in Frastaci v. Vapor Corporation (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 1389 also supports the granting of the motion and the fact that case is now on appeal does not alter its rationale. Although preemption is not plead as an affirmative defense, preemption is a matter of jurisdiction and the Court holds that it lacks jurisdiction to address these claims because they are preempted by federal law.
Regarding the claims based on exposure to Napa parts, plaintiff has presented no admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact for plaintiff fails to show how the testimony of Charles Allen is admissible. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
